titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles E. Fair to the acting Chief Secretary for Ireland, 21 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles E. Fair

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Charles E. Fair to the acting Chief Secretary for Ireland. Fair refers to his application for the vacant post of Clerk of Crown and Peace for County Westmeath which he submitted the previous week, enclosing two letters of testimony in support of his candidature and says that he will be sending more in due course.The office of Chief Secretary for Ireland was vacated by Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) on 3 May 1916. Birrell's replacement, Sir Henry Duke (1855-1939), took up the position from 31 July 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5615/10509

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
ATHLONE, 21st day of June, 1916. Sir,- I trust my Application forwarded to you last
week for Appointment of Clerk of the Crown and
Peace for County Westmeath has safely reached
you. I now enclose a couple of Testim-
onials from the managers of the Bank of Ireland
and Provincial Bank here. I hope to forward further Testim-
onials from The Right Honble Lord Castlemaine
and The Provost of Trinity College Dublin in
due course, I have the honor to be,
Your obedient servant, Charles. E. Fair The Chief Secretary (Acting),- Dublin Castle. I Add to file
II Ack.
III A.D. E.o.f
22:6:16. II Done RC 22/6
II Respd
Bank of Ireland Office Athlone 21st June 1916 I hereby Certify that I have known
Mr Char E O' Fair for the past
four years. During that time
and I believe for many years
previously Mr Fair has carried on
a large general Business as a Solicitor
and Land Agent in this town. Since that year 1913, when he
Succeeded to the business of the late
Mr P. Rob Kelly, he has acted as
local solicitor to this Bank, and
during this time he has throughly
satisfied the Bank Directors and
myself. In my option Mr Fair is
thoroughly competend to perform
the duties of Clerk of the Crown
and Peace for County Westmeath
if appointed to that office. O'Donovan agent.
Bank of Ireland
Provincial Bank of Ireland Limited Athlone 21st June. 1916. I have known Mr Charles E. Fair, for almost
the past six years ; and he has conducted the local
business of this Bank for 20 years. During the period of my management,
and I believe previously, he has always done so to the
satisfaction of my predecessors, myself, and the Bank's
Directors. In my opinion Mr. Fair is thoroughly
Competent, and well qualified to perform the duties
of Clerk of the Crown and Peace, for County Westmeath
if appointed to that office. W.F. Pearson, Manager.
